test.notes: Tests pass but some long-running tests (evidence_pipeline) timed out after 60s

lint.notes: Fixed formatting in knowledge_graph.rs before final check

blockingIssues.description: DashboardState::update_cost() is never called anywhere in the codebase at runtime. The cost tracking API will always return zero/nil values.
DashboardState::register_agent() is never called anywhere in the codebase at runtime. The /api/agents endpoint will always return an empty list.
DashboardState::broadcast_event() is never called anywhere in the codebase at runtime. WebSocket clients will connect but receive no events.

OtelConfig struct defines otlp_endpoint field but is never instantiated or used. There is no tracer provider setup, no OTLP exporter pipeline, and no mechanism to actually export traces to an endpoint.
GenAiSpanBuilder::start_span is marked #[allow(dead_code)] and is never called. The gen_ai attributes are defined but no actual spans are being created during LLM calls.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document port 3100 usage - the observability-dashboard-api feature uses port 3100 as the default dashboard port but this is not recorded in AGENTS.md's port range section. Add a line like 'Port 3100: Dashboard API (SWELL_DASHBOARD_PORT env override)'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: observability-dashboard-api sharedStateObservations: AGENTS.md says '3100-3199 reserved' but doesn't list actual port assignments

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add HTTP/WebSocket server patterns to the Rust skill - the skill documents async/tokio patterns but doesn't cover axum, tower-http, or WebSocket implementation. Workers implementing dashboard APIs encountered repeated compile errors with axum Router/State API.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: observability-dashboard-api: 'Transcript shows repeated cargo build failures with axum type errors, suggesting unfamiliarity with axum 0.7 API'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the relationship between LoopDetectionState (alerts.rs) and ReactLoopState (state_machine.rs). Two independent loop tracking mechanisms exist with no explanation of their relationship or which to use.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: observability-alerts sharedStateObservations: 'The transcript shows the worker explored ReactLoopState in state_machine.rs but implemented LoopDetectionState independently in alerts.rs. No shared state documentation explains the relationship'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add alert system design patterns and integration guidance to the Rust skill. Workers implementing alert functionality have to infer how AlertManager should integrate with the broader orchestrator flow.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: observability-alerts: 'Worker had to infer how alerts should integrate with the broader orchestrator flow'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document EventStore service - the correlation-ids feature implemented EventStore for querying correlation IDs but services.yaml doesn't mention it and AGENTS.md doesn't document it as a service.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: observability-correlation-ids: 'AGENTS.md doesn't document EventStore', 'services.yaml doesn't mention EventStore as a service'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that OpenTelemetry primitives in opentelemetry.rs are parallel implementation not unified with EventStore correlation IDs - two separate correlation systems exist and workers should be aware.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: observability-correlation-ids: 'OTel primitives in opentelemetry.rs are parallel implementation not unified with correlation IDs'
